title: quercus × beadlei
intro: Quercus × beadlei, also known as Beadle's oak or hybrid white oak, is a rare, deciduous tree hybrid between the white oak (Quercus alba) and the English oak (Quercus robur). The bark and leaves of Quercus × beadlei have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × beadlei are attributed to its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quercus × beadlei include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
